data.description:
Tracks GitHub issue navapbc/oscer#768.

Part of the exclusion/exception determination work under epic #732 (Data Source Hierarchy). Depends on #767 (and, transitively, #766 and #761).

## Background

`ExemptionDeterminationService` (renamed in #767) and `ExceptionDeterminationService` evaluate their conditions independently. When more than one condition applies, the member should receive the single most durable outcome. This issue evaluates the exclusion and exception conditions in one priority-ordered pass and records the most durable applicable result.

## User Story

As a Medicaid program processing a certification, I want all applicable exclusion and exception conditions considered together with the most durable one recorded, so that a member with multiple applicable conditions receives one correct, deterministic outcome.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Exclusion and exception conditions are evaluated in a single priority-ordered pass that records the most durable applicable outcome, using `Exclusion.priority_order`.
- [ ] All exclusions rank above all exceptions in the shared ordering.
- [ ] A check is not evaluated once no outcome it could produce would beat the current best result, so evaluation stops early.
- [ ] The correct event is published for the selected outcome, preserving existing audit-log writes.
- [ ] Existing single-condition outcomes are unchanged.

## Out-of-scope

- Sourcing signals from external data sources (#755, #756).
- Adding exclusion or exception checks for conditions not already implemented.

## Dependencies & Risks

- Depends on #767 (record the mandatory exception for a lapsed former exclusion, plus the service rename).
- Requires the shared month-intersection helper; coordinate with #760, which needs the same primitive.
- Ordering is configuration-driven (`Exclusion.priority_order`), so it can change later without code changes and does not need to be finalized before this work.

## Links & Resources

- Data Source Hierarchy tech spec, §"Exceptions from former exclusions" (linked from epic #732)
- Related: #767, #758 / #751 (priority configuration), #761, #760
data.title: Evaluate exclusion and exception conditions in a single priority-ordered pass
